Outputs: admitted entry ids; catalog roots; compatibility hashes
METRO.1.3(b) Compilation Hub: Intent → Certified Plan
Station: (\mathsf{ST_COMPILE})
Reference: (CHAPTER, 12, “12”) + (APPENDIX, E, “E”)
Role: goal normalization; constraints; deterministic routing; program emission; witness and ledger plan emission
Inputs: goal; context; constraints; metro map; corridor policy
Outputs: plan hash; feasibility certificates; explainability artifacts
METRO.1.3(c) Metro Compiler Hub: Deterministic Navigation Graph
Station: (\mathsf{ST_METRO_COMP})
Reference: (CHAPTER, 18, “18”)
Role: build/commit navigation graph; edge annotations; feasibility predicates; scoring/tie-break rules
Inputs: library root; corridor policy; verifier set; canon policy
Outputs: metro map hash; edge/node roots; membership proofs
METRO.1.4 Stability/repair/optimization stations (Ch9, Ch10, Ch16, Ch19)
METRO.1.4(a) Detector Hub: Drift/Defects/Evidence
Station: (\mathsf{ST_DETECT})
Reference: (CHAPTER, 9, “9”) + (APPENDIX, H, “H”)
Role: defect taxonomy; detector registry; evidence payloads; repair triggers
Inputs: step context; deltas; witnesses; policies
Outputs: evidence objects; mitigation obligations; defect localization
METRO.1.4(b) Repair Hub: Certified Bridges/Regrids/Renormalizers
Station: (\mathsf{ST_REPAIR})
Reference: (CHAPTER, 10, “10”)
Role: repair programs; repair receipts; minimal-bridge repairs; proof regeneration under stricter checks
Inputs: defect evidence; repair mapping; corridor feasibility
Outputs: repair plan; repair receipt; supersession updates
METRO.1.4(c) Optimization Hub: Proof-Carrying Rewrites
Station: (\mathsf{ST_OPT})
Reference: (CHAPTER, 16, “16”) + (CHAPTER, 15, “15”) + (APPENDIX, L, “L”)
Role: rewrite admissibility; proof-carrying equivalence; non-weakening; optimizer determinism
Inputs: program; rewrite rules; proof methods; corridor constraints
Outputs: optimized program; rewrite proofs; optimization receipts
METRO.1.4(d) Stability Hub: Checkpoints/Holonomy/Tunneling
Station: (\mathsf{ST_STABILITY})
Reference: (CHAPTER, 19, “19”)
Role: stability anchors; tolerance ledgers; loop diagnostics; certified tunneling jumps
Inputs: checkpoints; tolerance ledger; loop tests; bridge catalogs
Outputs: stability proofs; holonomy evidence; tunnel receipts
METRO.2 Lines by intent (fast routes)
Each line is a canonical “fast route” by intent. Lines specify an ordered sequence of stations and the required gates.
METRO.2.1 Line RUN: (Ch12 → Ch13 → Ch4 → Ch5 → AppD → Ch14)
METRO.2.1(a) Line definition.
Line: (\mathsf{LINE_RUN})
Intent: execute a goal to produce outputs + receipt
Stops (ordered):
(\mathsf{ST_COMPILE}) (Ch12/AppE)
(\mathsf{ST_LEDGER}) (Ch13/AppD)
(\mathsf{ST_KERNEL}) (Ch4/AppG)
(\mathsf{ST_WITNESS}) (Ch5/AppB)
(\mathsf{ST_LEDGER}) (AppD replay hooks embedded)
(\mathsf{ST_RECEIPT_ALG}) (Ch14 output semantics)
METRO.2.1(b) Required gates.
CHECKPOINT gate at entry (must have (\mathsf{Chk}_0) or build genesis checkpoint).
POLICY gate (runtime header hashes must match plan policy binds).
WITNESS gate (required witness plan satisfied).
EVIDENCE gate (mandatory detector evidence retained under (\Omega)).
METRO.2.1(c) Outputs.
plan hash (h(\mathsf{Plan}))
ledger header hash (h(\mathsf{LedHdr}))
terminal checkpoint hash (h(\mathsf{Chk}_n))
receipt hash (h(\mathsf{Rcpt})) and output roots (r_{\mathsf{pins}},r_{\mathsf{wits}},r_{\mathsf{ev}}?)
METRO.2.1(d) Success predicate.
replay verifies (\mathsf{OK}), outputs exist for requested targets, contradiction policy holds, tolerance ledger within budget.
METRO.2.2 Line VERIFY/PUBLISH: (Ch13 → Ch14 → AppM → AppN → AppO)
METRO.2.2(a) Line definition.
Line: (\mathsf{LINE_VERIFY_PUBLISH})
Intent: validate receipts; produce public verification bundle; conformance proof of verifier behavior
Stops:
(\mathsf{ST_LEDGER}) (Ch13/AppD replay verifier)
(\mathsf{ST_RECEIPT_ALG}) (Ch14 receipt validity/strength)
(\mathsf{ST_RECEIPT_FMT}) (AppM publication packages)
(\mathsf{ST_API}) (AppN for interface packaging constraints)
(\mathsf{ST_CONF}) (AppO conformance suite references)
METRO.2.2(b) Required gates.
POLICY gate: verifier set hash and canon policy hash must match receipt binds (or explicit bridge receipts).
DISCLOSURE gate (subtype of POLICY): if public mode required, disclosure bundle completeness must satisfy method disclosure policies.
EVIDENCE gate: publication class may require evidence roots (audit, forensic).
METRO.2.2(c) Outputs.
verified receipt validity artifact (\mathsf{RcptVerify})
publication package (\mathsf{PubPack}) with membership proofs and disclosed LCD objects
optional merged receipt for aggregations
METRO.2.2(d) Success predicate.
published package enables deterministic verification of claimed outputs under declared mode and policies.
METRO.2.3 Line OPTIMIZE: (Ch15 → Ch16 → AppL → AppO → Ch14)
METRO.2.3(a) Line definition.
Line: (\mathsf{LINE_OPTIMIZE})
Intent: produce optimized program/plan with proof-carrying rewrites and receipts
Stops:
(\mathsf{ST_MACRO}) (Ch15 macro system and deterministic compilation to opcode stream)
(\mathsf{ST_OPT}) (Ch16 proof-carrying rewrites)
(\mathsf{ST_PROOF_LIB}) (AppL method registry and proof schemas)
(\mathsf{ST_CONF}) (AppO optimizer/rewriter conformance)
(\mathsf{ST_RECEIPT_ALG}) (Ch14 substitution/refinement via receipts)
METRO.2.3(b) Required gates.
NON-WEAKEN gate (policy subtype): optimization safety contract must pass; no weakening of corridor constraints or exactness requirements.
CONVENTION gate: no implicit convention change; any convention change must be explicit bridges in replacement.
PROOF gate: rewrite proofs must verify at required verification level.
METRO.2.3(c) Outputs.
optimized program hash (h(\Pi'))
rewrite proof set root (r_{\mathsf{rw}})
optimization receipt (h(\mathsf{Rcpt}_{\mathsf{opt}}))
optional supersession artifacts for plan catalogs
METRO.2.3(d) Success predicate.
rewrite proofs validate equivalence under claim boundary and non-weakening; optimized plan is executable under (\Omega).
METRO.2.4 Line REPAIR/CONTROL: (Ch9 → Ch10 → Ch19 → AppH → AppI)
METRO.2.4(a) Line definition.
Line: (\mathsf{LINE_REPAIR_CONTROL})
Intent: diagnose defects; compile certified repair; enforce long-chain stability policies
Stops:
(\mathsf{ST_DETECT}) (Ch9 detector semantics; AppH evidence schemas)
(\mathsf{ST_REPAIR}) (Ch10 certified repair programs)
(\mathsf{ST_STABILITY}) (Ch19 checkpoints/tolerance/holonomy/tunneling)
(\mathsf{ST_DETECT}) (AppH repair completion evidence)
(\mathsf{ST_OMEGA}) (AppI corridor constraints; mitigation obligations)
METRO.2.4(b) Required gates.
DEFECT gate: must have defect evidence hash; repair mapping table must be resolvable and pinned.
CHECKPOINT gate: repair must start from stable boundary checkpoint or explicitly create one.
POLICY gate: repair must respect isolation sets and protected namespace rules.
METRO.2.4(c) Outputs.
repair plan hash (h(\mathsf{Plan}_{\mathsf{rep}}))
repair receipt (h(\mathsf{Rcpt}_{\mathsf{rep}}))
repair completion evidence (h(\mathsf{RepDone}))
optional supersession and index updates (governance-gated)
METRO.2.4(d) Success predicate.
defect evidence class no longer triggers at boundary; mandatory detectors return OK; repaired state is stable anchor when required.
METRO.3 Transfer rules (gates)
Transfers are admissibility checks when crossing station boundaries or switching lines. Gates are deterministic predicates and may emit required mitigation actions.
METRO.3.1 Checkpoint gates (Ch4, Ch13, Ch19, AppD)
METRO.3.1(a) Gate definition.
Gate: (\mathsf{GATE_CHK})
Kind: CHECKPOINT
Predicate: (\mathsf{ChkValid}(h(\mathsf{Chk}),h(\mathsf{Hdr}),h(\mathsf{LedHdr})))Passes iff:
checkpoint schema valid and canonical,
(h(\Psi)) recomputes from per-store hashes and header hashes,
ledger header hash matches execution context,
tolerance ledger hash matches if tracked.
METRO.3.1(b) Required artifacts.
checkpoint object (h(\mathsf{Chk}))
runtime header hash (h(\mathsf{Hdr}))
ledger header hash (h(\mathsf{LedHdr}))
METRO.3.1(c) Failure codes.
(\mathsf{E_CHK_MISSING}), (\mathsf{E_CHK_HASH}), (\mathsf{E_CHK_POLICY}) (pinned evidence codes)
METRO.3.1(d) Mitigation.If checkpoint invalid and (\Omega) permits stabilization, emit mitigation action id:
(\mathsf{ACT_STABILIZE_CHK}): run checkpoint-bound stabilization normalizers and post-commit audits, then re-create checkpoint.
METRO.3.2 Convention gates and bridge insertion (Ch7, Ch10, AppK)
METRO.3.2(a) Gate definition.
Gate: (\mathsf{GATE_SPEC})
Kind: CONVENTION
Predicate: (\mathsf{SpecCompat}(h(\mathsf{SpecOut}),h(\mathsf{SpecIn})))Passes iff (h(\mathsf{SpecOut})=h(\mathsf{SpecIn})). Otherwise requires explicit bridge chain.
METRO.3.2(b) Bridge insertion rule.If mismatch, compiler must insert bridge chain (B) such that:[h(\mathsf{SpecOut}) \xrightarrow{B} h(\mathsf{SpecIn}),]and insert mandatory post-bridge normalizers. Bridge selection is deterministic by pinned objective (exactness, tolerance, steps, witness burden, lex key).
METRO.3.2(c) Failure codes.
(\mathsf{CD_SPEC_MISMATCH}), (\mathsf{CD_MISSING_BRIDGE_CLASS}), (\mathsf{CD_MISSING_NORMALIZER})
METRO.3.2(d) Mitigation.Mandatory mitigation actions may include:
insert required normalizer,
reroute to avoid approximate bridge if exactness required,
halt if no feasible bridge chain exists under (\Omega).
METRO.3.3 Witness/evidence gates (Ch5, Ch9, AppB, AppH)
METRO.3.3(a) Gate definition.
Gate: (\mathsf{GATE_WIT})
Kind: WITNESS
Predicate: (\mathsf{WReqOK}(\mathsf{WPlan},W_t,\mathsf{Ctx}t,h{\mathsf{VerSet}}))Passes iff required witness types/method ids exist, verify, and bind to step context.
METRO.3.3(b) Evidence retention gate.
Gate: (\mathsf{GATE_EVID})
Kind: EVIDENCE
Predicate: (\mathsf{RetentionOK}(\Omega,E_t))Passes iff mandatory detector evidence is present and retained within caps; omissions only permitted for optional evidence under deterministic omission rules.
METRO.3.3(c) Failure codes.
(\mathsf{E_PROV}), (\mathsf{E_VERIFY}), (\mathsf{E_MISSING}), (\mathsf{E_EVID_OMIT})
METRO.3.3(d) Mitigation.
proof regeneration when required method support insufficient,
reroute to methods with feasible disclosure/cost classes,
escalate budgets only with explicit budget witnesses if permitted by (\Omega).
METRO.3.4 Policy compatibility gates (Ch3, Ch14, AppI, AppM)
METRO.3.4(a) Gate definition.
Gate: (\mathsf{GATE_POL})
Kind: POLICY
Predicate: (\mathsf{PolicyCompat}(h_\Omega,h_{\mathsf{Hdr}},h(\mathsf{Rcpt})))Passes iff:
receipt/policy hashes match required execution/verification hashes, or
explicit policy-bridge receipts exist and are consumed by dependency proofs when cross-policy compatibility is claimed.
METRO.3.4(b) Compatibility constraints.Policy compatibility includes:
canonicalization hash equality,
object standard hash equality,
verifier set hash equality,
corridor policy compatibility by strict equality unless explicit bridging exists.
METRO.3.4(c) Failure codes.
(\mathsf{RD_POLICY_MISMATCH}), (\mathsf{E_POLICY}), (\mathsf{E_DISCLOSE}) (when public mode required and missing)
METRO.3.4(d) Mitigation.
execute policy migration / policy bridge plan if supported and authorized,
re-execute under the required policy hash if bridging is not supported,
downgrade verification mode only if goal explicitly permits (encoded as new constraints hash and new receipt).
METRO.4 Deterministic extraction recipes
Each recipe is a canonical extraction procedure with explicit artifacts and gates. Recipes are the human-facing “how-to” but remain fully formal and executable by mapping steps to plan fragments.
METRO.4.1 New transform entry: (AppF → Ch11 → Ch12 → Ch13 → AppD → Ch14)
METRO.4.1(a) Inputs.
desired transform semantics expressed as lens in/out, SpecIn/SpecOut, invariant clauses, tolerance profile
implementation descriptor (program/macro/rule)
corridor policy (h_\Omega) and runtime header hash
method registry hash (h_{\mathsf{VerSet}})
METRO.4.1(b) Steps (deterministic).
Contract authoring (AppF): build (\mathsf{LT}) object with complete references and replay profile.
Admission packet (AppF): build (\mathsf{Admit}) with ref plan + ref receipt + tests + claims + dependency closure.
Library insertion (Ch11): pin entry under library namespace with contradiction control and governance authorization.
Metro rebuild (Ch18): rebuild metro map so routing sees the new edge.
Compilation check (Ch12/AppE): compile a route that uses the new entry under feasibility filters.
Execution (Ch13/AppD): run reference plan (or a canonical test plan) producing ledger and receipt.
Receipt algebra (Ch14): produce refinement/supersession artifacts if replacing older entries.
METRO.4.1(c) Outputs.
admitted entry id and library root update
admission receipt (h(\mathsf{RefRcpt}))
updated metro map hash
optional supersession record linking old/new entries
METRO.4.1(d) Success predicate.
admission receipt verifies; method disclosure/cost requirements satisfied; entry appears in metro map and is feasible under declared corridor class.
METRO.4.2 Diagnose drift and repair: (Ch9 → AppH → Ch10 → Ch13 → Ch14)
METRO.4.2(a) Inputs.
defect evidence hash (h(\mathsf{Evidence})) (convention drift, invariant drift, accumulation, replay divergence)
failing boundary marker (step index / edge id / checkpoint hash)
corridor policy (h_\Omega)
METRO.4.2(b) Steps (deterministic).
Evidence classification (Ch9/AppH): map defect code to repair class list using pinned mapping table.
Repair compilation (Ch10): compile minimal repair plan subject to (\Omega) budgets/scope and bridge availability.
Repair execution (Ch13/AppD): run repair plan from stable boundary checkpoint; emit repair receipt.
Completion proof (AppH): emit repair completion evidence binding defect evidence and post-repair detector OK-set.
Supersession/index update (Ch14/AppM): update indices/aliases if governance permits; otherwise publish repair receipt as dependency for downstream.
METRO.4.2(c) Outputs.
repair plan hash, repair receipt hash, repair completion evidence hash
optional supersession records and index updates
METRO.4.2(d) Success predicate.
defect no longer triggers; detectors OK; repaired state stable when required; downstream compositions reference repair receipt.
METRO.4.3 Compose and publish results: (Ch14 → AppM → AppN → AppO)
METRO.4.3(a) Inputs.
one or more receipt hashes
publication policy requirements (mode selective vs hash-only, required evidence retention class)
verifier context hash set
METRO.4.3(b) Steps (deterministic).
Receipt validation (Ch14): verify receipts under policy compatibility; compute refinement relations when required.
Dependency packing (AppM): build dependency proofs for reuse and composition; build composed receipts or merged receipts.
Public package build (AppM/AppN): build disclosure bundle meeting method disclosure policies; include membership proofs for outputs; include redaction markers if permitted.
Conformance link (AppO): include conformance receipts (or membership proofs) establishing verifier/canon correctness for the published policy hashes.
METRO.4.3(c) Outputs.
publication package hash (\mathsf{PubPack})
composed or merged receipt hash
index updates (optional, governance-gated)
METRO.4.3(d) Success predicate.
third-party verifier can deterministically validate claimed outputs and proofs under declared mode and policy hashes.
METRO.4.4 Extend library with plugin: (Ch11 → AppC → AppL → AppO → Ch18)
METRO.4.4(a) Inputs.
new lens family definitions (Appendix C)
new invariants and tolerances (Appendix C/J)
new proof methods/checkers (Appendix L)
governance permissions and corridor policy
METRO.4.4(b) Steps (deterministic).
Lens + invariant admission (AppC): pin lens objects, invariant clause objects, and measurement quantization policies.
Method admission (AppL): register method ids, LCD schemas, checker digests, verification-level policies; run conformance tests.
Conformance suite run (AppO): prove canonical bytes, verifier determinism, kernel determinism for plugin artifacts.
Library entry admission (Ch11/AppF): admit new transforms and bridges required for the new lens family.
Metro rebuild (Ch18): rebuild metro map so the new lens family and edges are routable.
METRO.4.4(c) Outputs.
updated verifier set hash and policy hashes (new runtime header universe)
conformance merged receipt
updated library root and metro map hash
METRO.4.4(d) Success predicate.
plugin artifacts are routable, executable, replay-verifiable, and conformance-certified under the new policy hashes.
APPENDIX METRO MAP — NAVIGATION AND EXTRACTION ROUTES
